local_terms: special improvement district; business improvement district; SID; BID
known_local_sources: HRS §46-80.5 Special improvement district (official text) [State of Hawaii] https://www.capitol.hawaii.gov/hrscurrent/Vol02_Ch0046-0115/HRS0046/HRS_0046-0080_0005.htm; Revised Ordinances of Honolulu Chapter 28 Special Improvement Districts (incl. Appendix 28-A) [City and County of Honolulu] https://codelibrary.amlegal.com/codes/honolulu/latest/honolulu/0-0-0-36151; Hawaiʻi County Code Chapter 35 Business Improvement Districts [County of Hawaiʻi] https://www.hawaiicounty.gov/home/showpublisheddocument/80/637649760877470000; Maui County Ordinance 4382 / Special Improvement Districts (Title 3 / Chapter ~3.70 enabling) [County of Maui] https://www.mauicounty.gov/DocumentCenter/View/106635/Ord-4382?bidId=; Waikīkī Business Improvement District (SID No. 1 / BID) [City and County of Honolulu / Waikīkī] https://waikikibid.org/; Downtown Honolulu Business Improvement District (expanded from Fort Street Mall SID No. 2) [City and County of Honolulu / Downtown] https://www.dohobid.com/; Waikīkī Beach Special Improvement District Association [City and County of Honolulu / Waikīkī Beach] https://www.wbsida.org/overview; Kailua Village Business Improvement District [County of Hawaiʻi / Kailua-Kona] https://historickailuavillage.com/; Fort Street Mall Business Improvement District Association (predecessor/related to Downtown) [City and County of Honolulu] https://fortstreetmall.org/fort-streets-history/fort-street-mall-bida/; Bill 51 (2025) Downtown Honolulu BID establishing ordinance materials [City and County of Honolulu] https://hnldoc.ehawaii.gov/hnldoc/measure/3401
next_action: Compile exhaustive list of active SIDs/BIDs by reviewing county ordinance appendices, clerk records, and recent bills for all four counties (focus Maui/Kauai for any active ones); locate any parcel or boundary GIS layers or maps from district management associations or county finance/tax offices; confirm no statewide registry via DCCA, DBEDT, or AG.
notes: Statewide enabling authority is HRS §46-80.5 (Special improvement district), allowing charter counties to enact ordinances creating SIDs for supplemental maintenance/security, environmental work, natural resources/hazards/climate adaptation, and other services/improvements that restore or promote business activity, with special assessments and optional bonds/association management (nonprofit under Ch. 414D). These are the legal equivalent of BIDs; Honolulu ROH Ch. 28 and Hawaii County Code Ch. 35 implement them (explicitly calling some BIDs); Maui Ord. 4382 (2016) adds enabling chapter for SIDs. Known active examples (Honolulu-centric): Waikiki SID/BID No. 1 (est. ~2000), Fort Street Mall SID No. 2 (expanded 2025 via Bill 51/Ord. to Downtown Honolulu BID), Waikiki Beach SID (2015). Hawaii County has Kailua Village BID under Ch. 35. Distinguish from: traditional improvement districts (HRS 46-80), community facilities districts (HRS 46-80.1), tax increment districts, zoning/design special districts (e.g., Honolulu zoning special districts GIS – different purpose), and HCDA improvement districts. No statewide registry, comprehensive official list, or unified GIS of BID/SID boundaries located; districts are local ordinances. Coverage is partial (authoritative enabling + major local codes + known district sites verified; full inventory of all active districts and boundaries incomplete). Failed or sparse searches for Kauai active districts and full statewide maps do not prove absence.

local_terms: Business Improvement District; BID; Local Business Improvement District
known_local_sources: Downtown Boise Business Improvement District / Downtown Boise Association [Boise] https://downtownboise.org/about/business-improvement-district; Nampa Business Improvement District [Nampa] https://www.cityofnampa.us/1253/Business-Improvement-District; Downtown Caldwell Business Improvement District / Destination Caldwell [Caldwell] https://www.cityofcaldwell.org/Business/Business-Improvement-District-Information; Downtown Idaho Falls Business Improvement District [Idaho Falls] https://downtownidahofalls.com/about-us/business-improvement-district/; Coeur d'Alene Downtown Business Improvement District / Downtown Association [Coeur d'Alene] https://cdadowntown.com/about/; Downtown Lewiston Business Improvement District [Lewiston] https://www.cityoflewiston.org/661/Business-Improvement-District; Historic Downtown Pocatello Business Improvement District [Pocatello] https://www.historicdowntownpocatello.com/explore-downtown; Idaho Statutes Title 50 Chapter 26 Business Improvement Districts (full chapter) [Statewide] https://legislature.idaho.gov/statutesrules/idstat/Title50/T50CH26/; Idaho Code 50-1703A Local Business Improvement Districts [Statewide] https://legislature.idaho.gov/statutesrules/idstat/Title50/T50CH17/SECT50-1703A/
next_action: Expand inventory via additional city municipal codes/ordinances, association directories (e.g., International Downtown Association, Association of Idaho Cities), and municipal GIS/open data portals for remaining cities (e.g., Twin Falls, Meridian status, Sandpoint current status); seek any boundary GeoJSON/shapefiles.
notes: Enabling authority verified as Idaho Code Title 50 Chapter 26 (Business Improvement Districts, authorized 1980, purposes include parking, public space improvements, events, transportation, retail promotion; special assessments on businesses/business property; cities may contract for administration). Distinct from broader Local Improvement Districts (LIDs, Title 50 Chapter 17 for capital infrastructure like streets/utilities, financed by assessments/bonds). Separate limited provision at 50-1703A (Local Business Improvement Districts, 1987) for exterior building improvements to match city architectural themes (requires 100% parcel owner consent, assessed as LIDs, max 20% of market value). No statewide registry, inventory, or central GIS of BIDs located after statute, government, and open-data searches (failed search ≠ none exist). Known active BIDs per official/district sources in Boise (est. ~1987), Nampa (~1985), Caldwell, Idaho Falls (~1990s, renewal 2027), Coeur d'Alene (1990), Lewiston (2020), Pocatello (1991); Sandpoint had one historically (dissolution attempt ~2010 failed; current status unclear/possibly inactive per some planning docs). Meridian has discussed/proposed but documents note operating without one. DBA notes 'at least six' in Idaho (likely undercount). Boundaries available as maps/PDFs on several district/city pages but no statewide or easily downloadable GeoJSON/ArcGIS layers confirmed. Distinctions from urban renewal/URDs and other special districts observed. Candidate sources are research leads only.

local_terms: Business Improvement District; BID; Special Service Area; SSA
known_local_sources: Business Improvement Districts (BID) and Special Service Areas (SSA) Program + Provider List [City of Chicago] https://www.chicago.gov/city/en/depts/dcd/supp_info/special_service_areassaprogram.html; Special Service Area (SSA) Provider List with maps [City of Chicago] https://www.chicago.gov/city/en/depts/dcd/supp_info/special_service_areasandproviderlist.html; Boundaries - Special Service Areas (GIS/open data) [City of Chicago] https://data.cityofchicago.org/Community-Economic-Development/Boundaries-Special-Service-Areas/cmr6-dn8c; SSA map / related SSA datasets [City of Chicago] https://data.cityofchicago.org/Community-Economic-Development/SSA-map/2k7v-9xvk; Special Service Areas [City of Peoria] https://www.peoriagov.org/1023/Special-Service-Areas; Special Service Area Districts (GIS) [Peoria County] https://data-peoriacountygis.opendata.arcgis.com/datasets/special-service-area-districts/explore; Special Service Area Tax District - Historical (GIS open data) [Cook County] https://hub-cookcountyil.opendata.arcgis.com/datasets/special-service-area-tax-district-historical-2023/about
next_action: Search additional municipal/county GIS portals and clerk records for SSAs outside Chicago metro (e.g., other large cities, IDOR tax extension contacts or county assessors); confirm any active BIDs under new law; locate fuller SSA statute compilation or DOR internal inventory if public.
notes: Illinois has two relevant tools. (1) Business Improvement District Law (65 ILCS 125/, P.A. 103-646, eff. 7-1-24) authorizes classic BIDs funded by district charges/assessments on property owners for services/improvements (marketing, security, cleaning, etc.). Applicability limited to municipalities with population exceeding 500,000 (i.e., Chicago only). Chicago DPD is accepting feasibility applications for 2027 BIDs; none appear active yet as law is recent. BIDs cannot overlap SSAs. (2) Longstanding equivalent is Special Service Areas under Special Service Area Tax Law (35 ILCS 200/27-5 et seq.), available statewide to municipalities and counties for special services/improvements funded by property taxes/levies only on the district (used extensively for BID-like downtown/business corridor services; Chicago alone has ~58 active SSAs with official lists, individual maps, citywide map PDF, and downloadable GIS boundaries). SSAs are created by local ordinance after notice/hearing/petition processes; ordinances and maps filed with county clerks/recorders. No central statewide public registry or inventory of all BIDs/SSAs located (DOR has historically tracked hundreds for tax purposes—e.g., >600 as of ~2011—but no public statewide list/GIS found). Distinguish from unrelated 'Business Districts' under 65 ILCS 5/11-74.3 (sales-tax financed redevelopment of blighted areas). Coverage strong for Chicago (authoritative lists + GIS); partial elsewhere via county GIS examples. Failed searches do not prove absence of districts. Use empty string for unsupported URLs.
